text.title: decomposition-react Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decomposition-react as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Over-splitting for cosmetics: splitting a 90-line cohesive form into five 20-line files because "the style guide says max 80 lines".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Premature splitting increases import noise and cognitive load without improving code. A 90-line cohesive form is more readable than five fragment files. Use the 80-line target as guidance, not a hard constraint.

failure-mode.description: Hooks that manage both form state and API calls, mixing input validation, submission, and error handling.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixing concerns makes hooks hard to test and reuse. Split into `useForm` (input state + validation) and `useSubmit` (mutation). Let React Hook Form handle the form part.

failure-mode.description: Storing API routes and pages in the same app/ structure without clear mental boundaries, mixing route handlers and page rendering logic.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: API routes (app/api/...) and pages (app/...) are conceptually different — one returns JSON, one returns HTML. Mixing them confuses static analysis and agent reasoning. Enforce a clear separation.
